response:
Sec. 1  
people who are allergic to peanuts cannot live underwater because their immune system reacts to peanut proteins by releasing histamines which can cause severe allergic reactions such as anaphylaxis which is life threatening and cannot be managed in an aquatic environment where medical assistance is not readily available and where the body's ability to process allergens is further complicated by the effects of water pressure and reduced oxygen availability  

Sec. 2  
living underwater requires a body to function in a different environment than on land which includes dealing with pressure changes temperature regulation and oxygen supply which are all factors that could exacerbate an allergic reaction and make it even more dangerous for someone with a peanut allergy to survive in such conditions without immediate access to antihistamines or epinephrine injections
